tomicActor ptolemy.actor.lib.Publisher All Implemented Interfaces: Actor , Changeable , java.lang.Cloneable, Debuggable , DebugListener , Derivable , Executable , Instantiable , ModelErrorHandler , MoMLExportable , Moveable , Nameable , java.io.Seria

MLExportable , Moveable , Nameable , java.io.Serializable, TypedActor public class Publisher extends TypedAtomicActor This actor publishes input tokens on a named channel. The tokens are "tunneled" to any instance of Subscriber that names the same c

o any instance of Subscriber that names the same channel and that is under the control of the same director. That is, it can be at a different level of the hierarchy, or in an entirely different composite actor, as long as the relevant composite a

mposite actor, as long as the relevant composite actors are transparent (have no director). It is an error to have two instances of Publisher using the same channel under the control of the same director. When you create a new Publisher, by defaul

ector. When you create a new Publisher, by default, it assigns a channel name that is unique. You can re-use channel names within opaque composite actors. This actor actually has a hidden output port that is connected to all subcribers via hidden

t that is connected to all subcribers via hidden "liberal links" (links that are allowed to cross levels of the hierarchy). Consequently, any data dependencies that the director might assume on a regular "wired" connection will also be assumed a

regular "wired" connection will also be assumed across Publisher-Subscriber pairs. Similarly, type constraints will propagate across Publisher-Subscriber pairs. That is, the type of the Subscriber output will match the type of the Publisher input

output will match the type of the Publisher input. Since: Ptolemy II 5.2 Version: $Id: Publisher.java,v 1.11.4.1 2006/10/27 19:14:05 cxh Exp $ Author: Edward A. Lee, Raymond A. Cardillo See Also: Serialized Form Accepted Rating: Red (cxh) Proposed R

s same channel will receive any transmissions to this port. This is a string that defaults to "channelX", where X is an integer that ensures that this channel name does not collide with a channel name already in use by another publisher. inpu

nel name already in use by another publisher. input public TypedIOPort input The input port. This base class imposes no type constraints except that the type of the input cannot be greater than the type of the output. output public TypedIOPort o

type of the output. output public TypedIOPort output The output port. By default, the type of this output is constrained to be at least that of the input. This port is hidden by default and the actor handles creating connections to it. _relati
